title: lathyrus sulphureus var. argillaceus
intro: Lathyrus sulphureus var. argillaceus, also known as the yellow peavine or prairie peavine, is a perennial wild pea plant that belongs to the Fabaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lathyrus sulphureus var. argillaceus include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant is typically prepared as a tea infusion or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
